perQueryEvaluations.query: What is the current YES price and implied probability on the 'Will Zohran Mamdani become President of the United States before 2045?' event on Kalshi?
quotedExcerpts: Based on the data from a past query (April 19, 2026), a specific market for **"Will Zohran Mamdani become President of the United States before 2045?"** was not found in the search results.
While the system identified a potential series match for **KXMAMDANIEO**, this likely refers to different political events or legislation rather than a presidential bid.
The execution of the search was partial and stopped before finding a terminal match for the specific Zohran Mamdani presidential market.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Tells buyer the specific market wasn't found and shows related political markets with prices, but fails to actually answer whether this market exists with a confident YES/NO. Suggests manual lookup as recourse.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Cites 'April 19, 2026' as date of query but no intra-day timestamps on the individual market prices shown.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Has specific cents prices (73¢, 70¢, 38¢, 9¢, 71¢) and dollar volumes ($1,021.30) for related markets, plus the KXMAMDANIEO series ticker.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: The alternative markets listed are trivially visible on the Kalshi politics page; no cross-entity or threshold-filtered insight a free LLM couldn't produce with a plain browse. Admits execution was partial.
scoreExplanations.completeness: The actual question (YES price + implied probability on the Mamdani market) is not answered. Response pivots to unrelated markets without decisively confirming the target market's existence.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: JD Vance markets all show $0.00 24h volume which is implausible for three simultaneous announcement markets; otherwise internally consistent. No numerical contradictions.

traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner query text is null; planner did not produce a structured plan to resolve the exact series/market via resolve_slug before falling back to free-text search.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: Only 2 search_markets calls issued, both returned the same KXMAMDANIEO series suggestion; no follow-through with resolve_slug({ slug: 'kxmamdanieo' }) or get_event/get_markets({ seriesTicker: 'KXMAMDANIEO' }) to enumerate children.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No completeness evaluations were performed (evaluations=[], completionChecks=0); the loop terminated after 2 tool calls without a completeness gate catching the missing resolution.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory shows search_markets called twice with nearly identical Politics-scoped queries and no follow-up call to resolve_slug or get_markets on the surfaced KXMAMDANIEO series. Trace shows toolStepCount=2, loopCount=2, and synthesis declaring 'execution was partial'.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Three JD Vance announcement markets all reporting $0.00 24h volume is suspicious — low liquidity is plausible but simultaneous zeros across three distinct tickers hint at stale 24h-volume fields.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: summary.loopCount=2 with no retries/self-heals and no resolve_slug follow-up despite the first search_markets surfacing KXMAMDANIEO as a candidate series — the iterative loop did not drill down.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Buyer gets an honest 'not found' with context but no decisive resolution and no attempt to confirm via the series ticker the tool itself surfaced.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: After search_markets surfaces a candidate series ticker, auto-chain resolve_slug + get_markets({ seriesTicker }) before concluding 'not found'.

diagnosticBrief.argsUsed: {"query":"Will Zohran Mamdani become President...","category":"Politics","maxSeriesScan":600} and {"query":"Zohran Mamdani president 2045","maxSeriesScan":1500}
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: search_markets → surfaces KXMAMDANIEO as 'potential series match' → loop terminates without drill-down
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: Response says 'system identified a potential series match for KXMAMDANIEO' but no subsequent tool call used that ticker.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: No evaluations executed (completeness.evaluations=[], completionChecks=0); no missingParts flagged.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Buyer would not pay again — the single surfaced lead (KXMAMDANIEO) was never followed up on and the target question remains unanswered.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Find all active Kalshi markets in the Elections category with YES implied probability between 10% and 40% and 24h volume above $10,000, ranked by 24h volume.
quotedExcerpts: As of April 19, 2026, there are **no active Kalshi markets** in the Elections category that currently meet all your criteria (YES implied probability between 10% and 40% and 24h volume above $10,000).
| Who will be Trump's next Attorney General? | 51% | $13,560.12 | Probability too high |
the execution loop was bounded and did not find any terminal matches for the exact $10,000 volume threshold within that specific probability window.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Gives a decisive 'none qualify' answer plus near-miss table, which is actionable for screening, but the caveat that the execution loop was bounded weakens confidence.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Dated April 19, 2026, but no per-market snapshot timestamp; relies on 24h volume freshness implicitly.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Specific volume ($13,560.12, $9,882.36, $2,016.65, $37.51) and probability (51%, 86%, 13%) values per near-miss market, with reasons for exclusion.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Cross-filter (probability band × 24h volume × category) is something the Kalshi UI can't trivially do, but the small near-miss set suggests either thin coverage or under-searching.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Answers the screening question with a null-result plus near-misses, but the bounded-loop caveat implies some Elections markets may have been missed.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: 'Will Trump say Thug before Apr 20, 2026?' being listed as an Elections/Politics near-miss is category-confused. Volume figures look internally consistent otherwise.

traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner query text null; planner did not decompose into 'filter by probability band' vs 'filter by volume' and did not issue a single parametric filter call.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: 20 tool calls / 12 loops: first get_markets_by_probability(coinflip, minLiquidity=10000) issued, then paginated get_markets 6 times (all returning cross-category/sports extended tickers per cursor content), then retried get_markets_by_probability(unlikely, coinflip) twice, then paginated get_events 6 times. Redundant pagination and never filtered to Elections series explicitly.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No completeness evaluations recorded (evaluations=[], completionChecks=0) despite 12 loops and bounded-execution disclaimer in synthesis.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory shows get_markets_by_probability called with probability='coinflip' (50%ish) rather than a 10%-40% band, and the 'unlikely' bucket retry does not match the explicit 10-40% window; the category filter 'Politics' is used instead of 'Elections' throughout.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Near-miss row 'Will Trump say Thug before Apr 20, 2026?' is a fun-market, not an Election — category tag hygiene issue in the surfaced set.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: toolCallHistory shows get_markets_by_probability args use the coarse 'coinflip'/'unlikely' enums rather than the precise 10-40% band the query requires, and category='Politics' is used when the query explicitly said 'Elections'.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Honest null-result with near-misses, but the underlying tool calls used wrong probability buckets and the wrong category tag, so the 'no markets qualify' conclusion is not trustworthy.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: When the query specifies a numeric probability band, map it to probability bucket 'unlikely' + 'coinflip' AND post-filter results by numeric yesPrice/100, rather than relying on a single enum; use category='Elections' when the user says 'Elections'.

diagnosticBrief.toolUsed: get_markets_by_probability (x3), browse_category (x2), get_markets (x6), get_events (x6), get_all_series, discover_trending_markets, get_all_categories
diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: get_markets_by_probability with probability='unlikely' + client-side numeric filter on yesPrice in [10,40] and volume24h>10000, scoped to category='Elections'
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: get_markets paginated 6x into cross-category and sports-extended tickers (cursor payloads show KXMVECROSSCATEGORY and KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED), wasting budget.
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: Near-miss rows include 'Will Trump say Thug before Apr 20, 2026?' and 'Will Nigel Farage be the next UK PM?' — not all Elections-proper.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: No evaluations; no missingParts; no needsDifferentTools.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Buyer is on the fence — they get a null-result with receipts, but the tool-argument mismatch (wrong category, wrong probability bucket) makes the 'no markets qualify' conclusion shaky.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Screen the Kalshi Economics category for active markets whose orderbook depth can absorb a $5,000 YES order at <= 2 cents of slippage, and list the top 5 by available depth.
quotedExcerpts: Based on the data from a past query on Sunday, April 19, 2026, I could not identify any active markets in the **Economics** category that meet your criteria.
Category discovery tools returned **zero active markets** for Economics at the time of the search.
Because no markets were surfaced as active in the Economics category, orderbook slippage (<= 2 cents) could not be calculated.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Tells buyer 'zero active markets in Economics' which is a dead-end answer and inconsistent with the series list the response itself shows (KXFED, CPIYOY, KXUSRETAIL, etc.).
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Has dated query reference (April 19, 2026) but no live orderbook timestamps.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Lists series tickers (KXFED, CPIYOY, KXUSRETAIL, KXHOME, GDPCN, CPISHELTER, KXBALANCESHEET) with labels, but no numeric market-level data.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: No slippage computation performed; a free LLM can also state 'Kalshi has a Fed funds rate series' without tools.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Completely fails the task: no depth analysis, no top-5 list, no slippage calculation, and the null-result is internally contradicted by the series list.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Red flag: response asserts 'zero active markets' in Economics but then enumerates seven Economics series — this is internally contradictory. CPI/Fed markets are known to be active on Kalshi.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: analyze_market_liquidity was available in selectedMethods but was not called — the exact tool for the task is never invoked.
traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner query text null; plan did not include orderbook-depth analysis or per-ticker slippage computation.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: 9 get_markets / browse_category / get_all_series / discover_trending_markets calls, none of which probe orderbook depth; 6 of 9 calls are paginated get_markets that kept walking through cross-category/sports tickers rather than filtering to Economics seriesTicker.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No completeness evaluations (evaluations=[], completionChecks=0); a quality gate should have flagged 'no orderbook probe attempted for a slippage question'.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory shows zero calls to get_market_orderbook or analyze_market_liquidity despite the query explicitly asking about depth / slippage. Pagination walked into KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED tickers (see cursor payloads) even though category='Economics' was set.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Response claims 0 active markets yet lists 7 known-active Economics series (KXFED, CPIYOY, etc.) — internal contradiction.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: analyze_market_liquidity and get_market_orderbook were available in toolSchemas / selectedMethods but toolCallHistory contains only discovery tools; the slippage computation tool was never called.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Buyer asked for a quantitative depth screen and got a discovery failure with a self-contradictory null-result.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: For slippage/depth queries, after discovering candidate Economics markets (via browse_category + get_all_series), always iterate get_market_orderbook (or analyze_market_liquidity) on each ticker and compute $-depth per cent.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: analyze_market_liquidity or get_market_orderbook per Economics market ticker, with depth computation to absorb $5K at <=2¢
diagnosticBrief.argsShouldHaveUsed: After discovery, loop get_market_orderbook({"ticker":"<KXFED-...>","depth":20}) per candidate and compute cumulative $ depth at each cent offset
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: browse_category(Economics, sortBy=liquidity) → get_all_series(Economics) → discover_trending_markets(Economics) → get_markets(Economics) pagination into cross-category tickers; no orderbook probe issued.
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: Response section 'Identified Economics Series (Inactive/No Open Markets)' lists 7 series while header says 0 active markets — discovery surfaced series but tooling never enumerated their child markets.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Buyer feels ripped off — the question demanded orderbook-depth math and no depth tool was ever called, while the null-result contradicts the response's own series list.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Compare the current YES prices and orderbook liquidity across the top 5 candidates in the 'Who will be the next new Speaker of the House?' event (KXNEXTSPEAKER-31) on Kalshi, and identify which candidate's pricing looks most out of line relative to its liquidity.
quotedExcerpts: As of April 19, 2026, the "Who will be the next new Speaker of the House?" (KXNEXTSPEAKER-31) event on Kalshi contains only 3 active candidate markets rather than 5.
Based on the current orderbook data, **Jim Jordan** appears to have the pricing most out of line relative to his liquidity

scoreExplanations.actionability: Picks a specific candidate (Jim Jordan) as the mispriced one, gives concrete comparison (tight 3¢ spread, higher top-5 ask depth than Jeffries despite 13¢ price) — directly tradeable thesis.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Dated April 19, 2026; per-market orderbook snapshot implied (fresh orderbook from get_market_orderbook).
scoreExplanations.specificity: Every row has YES, best bid, best ask, spread, top-bid qty, top-ask qty, bid depth top-5, ask depth top-5 — exact numbers throughout.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Cross-candidate depth comparison inside a single event with a mispricing conclusion — the Kalshi UI doesn't do this and a free LLM absolutely can't.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Acknowledges only 3 of requested 5 candidates exist (legitimate data limit), supplies the mispricing conclusion the query asked for. Minor ding because it doesn't explicitly report that top-5 is unachievable at source.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Fractional quantities like 377.78 and 1,688.78 are unusual for a contract-count orderbook (usually integers). Not impossible if partial fills were displayed, but it's a modest red flag.

traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No completeness evaluations; would have been nice to flag 'event has <5 candidates' explicitly via a repair step.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: Clean 4-call pattern: get_event(KXNEXTSPEAKER-31) then 3× get_market_orderbook across the 3 child markets — exactly the right shape.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Fractional orderbook quantities (377.78, 1,688.78) are atypical for a cents-denominated binary; could be stale synthetic fills but worth flagging.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: toolCallHistory shows get_market_orderbook(depth=N, preferFixedPoint=true) across 3 markets; response surfaces non-integer top-ask quantities (377.78, 1688.78), suggesting a unit/conversion mismatch in the orderbook parser.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Strong delivery of the requested analysis with a concrete mispricing call; only blemish is the odd fractional quantities in the orderbook display.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: In the orderbook formatter, round or explain non-integer contract counts; or verify whether the API is returning cents-weighted dollars vs contract counts and label the column accordingly.

diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: get_event(KXNEXTSPEAKER-31) → 3 child markets → get_market_orderbook per child → synthesize depth/spread table
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: Top-ask qty = 377.78 for Jeffries and 1,688.78 for top-5 ask depth — fractional units in a normally integer contract-count field.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Buyer would pay again — crisp answer, clean trace, only nitpick is the fractional orderbook quantities.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Compare 30-day YES-price trajectories on 'Will OpenAI or Anthropic IPO first?' (KXOAIANTH-40) and 'Will Ramp or Brex IPO first?' (KXRAMPBREX-40). Which event has re-priced more over the last 30 days, in which direction, and by how many cents?
quotedExcerpts: Based on data from a past query (as of April 19, 2026), the event **'Will OpenAI or Anthropic IPO first?' (KXOAIANTH-40)** has re-priced significantly more over the last 30 days compared to the Ramp vs. Brex market.
| Will OpenAI or Anthropic IPO first? | `KXOAIANTH-40-OAI` | 60¢ | 37¢ | **23 cents** | Downward |
The execution loop for this request was intentionally bounded, exhausting its step budget before returning a terminal result.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Concrete verdict: OAI leg re-priced 23¢ down, Ramp 8¢ up — directly usable as a relative-value signal.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Mar 20, 2026 baseline vs Apr 19, 2026 current — explicit 30-day window timestamps.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Exact cents deltas (60→37, 86→94), resolved child tickers (KXOAIANTH-40-OAI, KXRAMPBREX-40-RAMP), direction labeled.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: 30-day trajectory reconstruction from trade history across two event pairs is non-trivial; a free LLM cannot produce this.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Admits the execution loop was 'intentionally bounded, exhausting its step budget'; values are 'synthesized from partial trade history', which undermines trust in the reported deltas.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Red flag: response says figures are 'synthesized from partial trade history', the trace shows initial get_market on KXOAIANTH-40/KXRAMPBREX-40 failed ('Market not found'), and only the -OAI / -RAMP child legs were used. Reporting a single-leg price as the event-level re-pricing may mis-represent the event.

traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner query text null; did not plan to use get_market_candlesticks on the correct child market tickers from the start, nor to pick a single representative leg per event.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: 20 tool calls / 12 loops: 2 candlestick calls on top-level event tickers returned unusable data (resLen 123-124 bytes), 2 get_market calls failed with 'not found' (tool response even told the executor to use resolve_slug first), recovery via search_markets + resolve_slug eventually succeeded, then 10× get_market_trades pagination to reconstruct 30-day price series.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No completeness evaluations recorded; synthesis disclosed 'step budget exhausted' but no self-heal/repair event fired.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory: get_market({"ticker":"KXOAIANTH-40"}) → error 'Market not found... The ticker may have been incorrectly constructed. Try get_event_by_slug({slug:"kxoaianth-40"}) first'. The executor eventually recovered via resolve_slug but burned ~4 calls doing so. Then 10 get_market_trades pagination calls consumed most of the remaining budget.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: failureCount=2 in summary. Only the -OAI and -RAMP legs are probed; the event-level re-pricing claim is extrapolated from one leg. Reported 30-day prices are reconstructed from trades (mid approximations), not candlesticks.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: toolCallHistory shows get_market called on event tickers KXOAIANTH-40 and KXRAMPBREX-40 (which are event-level not market-level) before resolve_slug was used; the tool itself returned an error explicitly recommending resolve_slug first.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Answer is plausible and specific, but the trace shows 4 wasted calls chasing ticker-shape errors before the correct child legs were resolved, and the step budget ran out mid-reconstruction.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: On any query mentioning an event ticker (ending with -NN like -40, -30), default-chain resolve_slug or get_event({ eventTicker }) before attempting get_market; do not call get_market_candlesticks on an event ticker.

diagnosticBrief.toolUsed: get_market_candlesticks (x2 wasted), get_market (x2 failed on event tickers), search_markets (x2), resolve_slug (x2), get_market (x2 on -OAI/-RAMP), get_market_trades (x10 paginated)
diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: get_event + resolve_slug FIRST to obtain child market ticker, then get_market_candlesticks({"ticker":"<child>","periodInterval":1440,"startTs":<30d-ago>,"endTs":<now>})
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: get_market error: 'Market KXOAIANTH-40 not found. Possible issues: 1. The ticker may have been incorrectly constructed... Try get_event_by_slug({ slug: kxoaianth-40 }) first'
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: {"error":"Market 'KXOAIANTH-40' not found. Possible issues: 1. The ticker may have been incorrectly constructed (don't add suffixes like -001) 2. Use the exact 'ticker' value from get_event_by_slug or search_markets results 3. Try get_event_by_slug({ slug: \"kxoaianth-40\" }) first to get the correct ticker"}
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: No evaluations; completionChecks=0 despite the 'step budget exhausted' disclaimer in synthesis.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Buyer is on the fence — the answer is specific and directional, but the trace wasted 4 calls on wrong-shape tickers and ran out of budget reconstructing prices from trades.

perQueryEvaluations.query: For the 'Who will be named as Xi Jinping's successor?' event (KXXISUCCESSOR-45JAN01), identify the candidate sub-market that best combines high 24h volume, tight bid-ask spread, and positive 7-day YES-price drift. Which successor is the 'smart-money plus momentum' pick right now?
quotedExcerpts: As of April 19, 2026, based on the market data for the event **"Who will be named as Xi Jinping's successor?" (KXXISUCCESSOR-45JAN01)**, the "smart-money plus momentum" pick is **Li Qiang**.
Here is the complete returned collection for Who will be named as Xi Jinping's successor? (14 items).

scoreExplanations.actionability: Names Li Qiang as the pick, but without showing the 24h volume, 7-day drift, or spread numbers the query explicitly asked for, the buyer can't verify or size the trade.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Dated April 19, 2026; no candlestick/trade timestamp for the 7-day drift computation is surfaced.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Table shows Best Ask, Best Bid, No Price per candidate — but the three requested metrics (24h volume, spread numerically, 7-day drift) are not listed per candidate.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: The candidate list + prices is exactly what the Kalshi event page shows. The promised cross-metric ranking is not actually displayed, so the unique value-add doesn't materialize for the buyer.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Fails to report the three metrics the query explicitly named (24h volume, spread, 7-day drift) per candidate, even though the trace shows 14 get_market_orderbook and 14 get_market_candlesticks calls were made.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: YES+NO sums are inconsistent (e.g., 27+77=104, 5+98=103, 15+86=101, 11+98=109) across candidates — either these are ask+ask (not bid+ask) or there's a formatting mismatch. Worth flagging.

traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner query text null; planner did not encode that the final output must include per-candidate 24h volume, spread, and 7-day drift columns.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: 29 tool calls: get_event → 14× get_market_orderbook → 14× get_market_candlesticks. Data is collected but synthesis discards the volume/spread/drift columns and surfaces only ask/bid/no prices.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No completeness evaluations fired; a completeness gate should have caught 'response names Li Qiang but doesn't show the 3 metrics the query asked for'.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory shows 14 orderbook + 14 candlestick calls across all successor candidates — the data was fetched but not surfaced. This is a synthesis_issue: the LLM synthesizer collapsed the table to 4 columns and omitted the very metrics the query named.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: YES+NO sums across candidates are inconsistent with a normal Kalshi binary (should be ~100¢): 5+98=103, 11+98=109, 3+99=102 — suggests the 'No Price' column is actually best-ask-NO which can legitimately be >1-YES-bid, but it's not explained to the buyer.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: toolCallHistory shows 14 candlestick + 14 orderbook calls (enough data for all three requested metrics), but the rendered table only shows Best Ask/Best Bid/No Price and omits 24h volume, spread, and 7-day drift.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Execution gathered the right data but synthesis dropped the critical columns and offered the verdict as a bare assertion; buyer has no way to audit.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: In synthesis, enforce that every metric named in the query must appear as a column in the result table before naming a winner.

diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: Tool data includes candlesticks → compute 7d drift per candidate; orderbook → compute spread + 24h vol; but synthesis omits these columns.
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: 14 candlesticks + 14 orderbooks collected; rendered table has only 4 columns (Name, Best Ask, Best Bid, No Price) and no volume/drift.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: No evaluations; no missingParts flagged — completeness gate did not catch the dropped columns.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Buyer is disappointed — the right data was fetched, but the three metrics the query named are absent from the answer, so 'Li Qiang' is an unsupported claim.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Using only Kalshi data, find any candidate sub-market inside 'Who will be the next Prime Minister of the UK?' (KXNEXTUKPM-30) where best YES-ask + best NO-ask is strictly less than 100 cents (implied within-leg arbitrage). Report the leg, the YES-ask, the NO-ask, and the arbitrage edge in cents.
quotedExcerpts: Based on Kalshi data as of **April 19, 2026**, there are currently **no sub-markets** within the "Who will be the next Prime Minister of the UK?" event (KXNEXTUKPM-30) where the sum of the best YES-ask and best NO-ask is strictly less than 100 cents.
Across all 20 candidates checked, the "edge" remains negative, indicating that the markets are currently efficient and do not offer a guaranteed within-leg arbitrage profit at these specific prices.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Clean 'no arb' verdict with closest-to-parity table; directly actionable (buyer knows not to trade and sees which candidates are closest).
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Dated April 19, 2026; no per-orderbook timestamp cited.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Per-candidate YES-ask, NO-ask, sum, and edge in cents — exact numbers.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Within-leg arb sweep across 20 candidates in a single event is not doable on the Kalshi UI and not something a free LLM can fabricate.
scoreExplanations.completeness: All 20 candidates 'checked' per the summary; null-result accompanied by near-parity evidence.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Red flag: 6 of 8 shown candidates have IDENTICAL values (1.0¢ YES-ask, 99.9¢ NO-ask, 100.9¢ sum, -0.9¢ edge) — uniform values across distinct entities. May be legit (longshot floor), but the exact duplication across Andrew Tate, Ed Davey, Boris Johnson, James Cleverly, Robert Jenrick, and Darren Jones is suspicious.

traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No completeness evaluations; would have been nice to flag the uniform-1.0¢/99.9¢ pattern as a potential data-floor artifact.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: Clean execution: get_event(KXNEXTUKPM-30) + 20× get_market_orderbook — exactly the right shape for this query.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Six of eight displayed rows share identical (1.0¢, 99.9¢, 100.9¢, -0.9¢) — uniform-value red flag across distinct candidates. Response should either explain this as a minimum-tick floor or verify independently.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: toolCallHistory shows 20 distinct get_market_orderbook calls, yet the rendered table shows 6 candidates with identical penny/99.9¢ values — the underlying data or its extractor is collapsing longshot orderbooks to a default floor.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Execution pattern is textbook-clean and the answer is decisive, but the suspicious uniform values across 6 distinct longshot candidates deserve explicit acknowledgment.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: When many candidates share identical floor prices, add a note distinguishing 'true minimum-tick orderbook' from 'no-bid fallback' so the buyer knows the edge calculation isn't artifactual.

diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: get_event → 20 child tickers → get_market_orderbook per child → compute YES-ask + NO-ask vs 100¢
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: 6 of 8 displayed candidates return (bestYesAsk=1.0, bestNoAsk=99.9) — identical across Tate/Davey/Johnson/Cleverly/Jenrick/Jones.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Buyer would probably pay again — answer is decisive and well-supported, but the uniform-penny values across 6 distinct longshots should be footnoted to avoid looking artifactual.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Build a risk-diversified 5-market Kalshi shortlist of actively trading contracts priced between 15% and 40% YES where each market (a) has orderbook depth sufficient for a $2,500 YES position at <= 3 cents of slippage and (b) has 7-day cumulative volume >= $50K. The shortlist must include at least one market from Elections, one from Economics, and one from Science and Technology.
quotedExcerpts: As of Sunday, April 19, 2026, the search for a risk-diversified 5-market shortlist was **partially completed**.
| `KXECONSTATCPI-26APR-T0.4` | CPI month-over-month in Apr 2026? | 29 (29%) | Economics | $14,324.19 |
The execution loop exhausted its step budget before identifying qualifying markets for **Elections** and **Science and Technology**.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Produces only 1 of 5 requested markets and even that one fails the $50K 7-day volume bar ($14,324.19). Buyer cannot act on this shortlist.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Has a specific ticker and price for one market and series counts (1,268 Elections, 203 Sci/Tech), but no slippage math and no depth data.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Cross-category screen is something the Kalshi UI can't do, but the screen didn't finish, so the unique value isn't delivered.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Delivers 1/5 markets, no depth computation, no qualifying Elections or Science/Tech candidate, and recommends the buyer relax the thresholds — effectively a failure.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: The single surfaced market's volume ($14,324.19) is at least internally consistent with the 'below $50K' disclaimer. Claim that high-volume activity is dominated by 'Unknown' / sports extended tickers is plausible but unverified.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: analyze_market_liquidity never called despite the query demanding slippage depth.
traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner query text null; plan did not partition the task as 'category-by-category screen + depth probe per candidate + 7d volume filter', leading to heavy redundant discovery pagination.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: 31 tool calls / 12 loops: 3× get_markets_by_probability, 3× browse_category repeated, 2× get_all_series, 2× discover_trending_markets, 12× get_markets paginating through cross-category/sports extended tickers, 0× get_market_orderbook or analyze_market_liquidity. Almost all budget spent on discovery redundancy.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No completeness evaluations; a quality gate should have fired 'missing 4/5 markets, no depth probe executed' and triggered repair or different-tools.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory shows 12 get_markets pagination calls across the same cross-category cursors, and 0 orderbook calls — the slippage half of the query was never attempted. 12 loops consumed without category-specific coverage for Elections or Sci/Tech.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Claim '1,268 Elections and 203 Sci/Tech series exist but no qualifying markets' is unsubstantiated since no per-series enumeration was performed in those two categories.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: summary.toolCalls=31, loopCount=12, with no retryCount/selfHealCount ticks, and toolCallHistory shows repeated browse_category/get_markets_by_probability on the same category set — the iterative loop did not diversify into Elections/Sci-Tech specifically or call orderbook tools.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Buyer asked for a 5-market diversified shortlist and got 1 partial market with an explicit 'we ran out of steps' disclaimer.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Partition the budget by category (Elections / Economics / Sci-Tech) up front and probe orderbook depth per candidate rather than re-running discovery tools on the same category 3×.

diagnosticBrief.toolUsed: get_all_categories, get_markets_by_probability (x6), browse_category (x6), get_all_series (x2), discover_trending_markets (x3), get_markets (x12)
diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: Per-category browse_category + get_markets_by_probability ONCE, then get_market_orderbook on each shortlisted candidate to compute $2,500-at-3¢ depth, with a separate pass for 7-day cumulative volume via get_market_candlesticks or a market-level field
diagnosticBrief.argsUsed: repeated {"category":"Economics"|"Elections"|"Science and Technology","status":"open","sortBy":"volume_24h","limit":100} with cursor pagination into cross-category tickers
diagnosticBrief.argsShouldHaveUsed: One browse_category per category, then per-candidate get_market_orderbook({"ticker":"<ticker>","depth":20,"preferFixedPoint":true}) and slippage calculation
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: 12x get_markets with cursors walking into KXMVECROSSCATEGORY and KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED despite category filter — pagination not honoring category.
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: Single surfaced market KXECONSTATCPI-26APR-T0.4 at 29% / $14,324.19 — below the $50K threshold the query required.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: No evaluations; needsDifferentTools never fired despite the clear gap.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Buyer feels ripped off — the shortlist is 1/5 and doesn't satisfy the volume bar, with zero depth computation.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Identify the 3 Kalshi markets closing within the next 14 days with (a) cumulative 30-day volume > $100K and (b) the largest absolute gap between current YES midpoint and the 30-day implied-probability moving average. Report each market's current price, 30-day mean, gap in cents, and direction (rich vs cheap vs mean).
quotedExcerpts: The requested analysis of Kalshi markets could not be completed because the specific 30-day metrics required—cumulative volume and implied-probability moving averages—were not provided in the tool results.
the highest total volume reported for a single candidate was 2,283 (Ticker: `KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED-S20261B1A49A3E9B-9BA82F0109F`), which does not meet the $100,000 threshold.

To identify significant price gaps, you may want to focus on high-liquidity categories such as "Economics" or "Politics" and consider a lower volume threshold (e.g., >$1,000) for more frequent matches.

scoreExplanations.actionability: No markets identified, no gaps computed, no direction labels — buyer gets zero trade ideas.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: References May 3, 2026 as close window and 'Sunday, April 19, 2026' implicit but minimal.
scoreExplanations.specificity: One esports ticker with volume 2,283 and a close-window date — otherwise nothing specific.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Any free LLM can say 'focus on Economics or Politics and lower your threshold'. No value-add.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Fails every part of the request: no 3 markets, no current price, no 30-day mean, no gap, no direction.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: The claim that 'highest total volume for any single candidate was 2,283' is implausible for Kalshi (high-liquidity markets routinely exceed $100K). Likely the wrong volume field was read from the market payload.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: get_market_candlesticks (for 30-day implied-probability moving average) and get_market_trades were never called; analyze_market_sentiment / check_market_efficiency also unused.
traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner query text null; plan did not decompose the query into (1) filter by close_ts within 14d, (2) enrich each with 30d candlesticks, (3) compute gap — it only issued 6 get_markets pagination calls.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: 6 get_markets calls, 1 loop, 1 toolStep — the loop terminated after one discovery pass with zero candlestick/volume enrichment.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No completeness evaluations; a completeness gate should have flagged 'query asks for 30-day MA which requires candlesticks, none fetched'.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory shows 6 paginated get_markets calls and terminates; the tool field read for 'total volume' appears to have been a per-market contract count (max 2,283) rather than cumulative $-volume, hence the implausibly low cap claim.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Max volume 2,283 being presented as the ceiling across 'hundreds of active markets' is implausible — likely contract-count vs dollar-volume confusion in the extractor.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: toolCallHistory contains only get_markets (x6) with zero get_market_candlesticks, zero get_market_trades, and zero analyze_* calls, despite the query explicitly requiring 30-day price history (a candlestick/trades job).
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Buyer gets a non-answer with a suspicious volume ceiling. None of the required metrics (current price, 30-day mean, gap, direction) appear.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: When the query names a time-window average (30-day MA), default-call get_market_candlesticks({"periodInterval":1440,"startTs":<now-30d>,"endTs":<now>}) per candidate; verify the 'volume' field represents $ not contract count.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: get_markets with maxCloseTs filter to 14d-out → then get_market_candlesticks(periodInterval=1440, startTs=now-30d) per candidate → compute 30d mean and gap vs current YES midpoint
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: summary.loopCount=1, toolStepCount=1 → single discovery pass, no candlestick enrichment.
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: Response cites max volume 2,283 on an esports MVE ticker, implying the extractor used contract-count not $-volume.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: No evaluations; no needsDifferentTools signal despite obvious gap.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Buyer feels ripped off — the pipeline made one discovery pass, never fetched any historical price data, and the surfaced volume ceiling is implausibly low.

topImprovementLevers: When a query mentions historical windows (7-day, 30-day, trajectory) or event-level tickers (KX...-NN), default-chain get_market_candlesticks and resolve_slug/get_event BEFORE get_market / search_markets, so the runtime stops wasting budget on discovery when time-series or ticker-resolution is the real need.

When a query names specific output metrics (24h volume, bid-ask spread, 7-day drift, slippage, 30-day MA), enforce in synthesis that every named metric appears as a column in the result, and trigger a completeness-repair loop if a metric was fetched but dropped (as in kalshi-013).

Partition discovery budget by category when the query demands cross-category coverage (kalshi-016) and always call get_market_orderbook / analyze_market_liquidity when the query asks for depth or slippage (kalshi-008, kalshi-016) instead of looping get_markets pagination into cross-category and sports-extended tickers.
